name: The Eshbrīn
summary: The Eshbrīn are Epēshu's populist faction, and by far the dominant power in the senate since Hethel of the Mālaz clan won the last election for Surīhurūk. With the city's army away on campaign under her rivals, Hethel has moved to consolidate her rule: expanding the grain dole despite regional famine, swelling the ranks of her personal guard, and — so her critics whisper — laying the groundwork for outright tyranny.

When the storm and the citywide failure of blood glyphs struck Epēshu, Pahīn named questioning the Surīhurūk among the party's chief leads — though the Surīhurūk rarely grants private audiences, and non-senators are forbidden even to speak before the Curia.

members.name: Hethel
members.role: Surīhurūk (governor-consul) of Epēshu; leader of the Eshbrīn, of the Mālaz clan

name: The Orondēs
summary: The Orondēs are the conservative faction that lost the senate to the Eshbrīn in the last election. They look to Pebros, commander of Epēshu's army, as their informal leader; he has been away on campaign but is now returning, presently encamped with his forces among the ruins of Lepon some fifty miles from the city.
Should Pebros arrive with the army's loyalty still his own, the Orondēs may use that strength to wrest back control of the senate — a prospect that has the Eshbrīn visibly anxious to forestall.

members.name: Pebros
members.role: commander of Epēshu's army; informal leader of the Orondēs, camped at the ruins of Lepon

name: The Lektān Priesthood
summary: The Lektān are the priesthood of the Blue Temple on Epēshu's northern coast, devoted to Thobrauk the Pilot, the city's patron deity. Led by the Tuk Lektān (high priest) Ishkibal, they hold considerable public sway and lean toward the Orondēs, since the general Pebros is known for his piety.

Ishkibal has declared the unrelenting storm and the citywide failure of blood glyphs to be a divine omen, promising that 'great things' will come to pass this Yuleday, now only days away — a claim the party has flagged as a lead worth investigating in its own right.

members.name: Ishkibal
members.role: Tuk Lektān (high priest) of the Blue Temple; proclaims the storm and glyph-failures omens of Yuleday

name: The Senate of Epēshu
summary: Epēshu is governed by a body of one hundred senators, presided over by an elected Surīhurūk ('governor, consul') who stands for re-election every two years. The senate convenes in the Curia overlooking the Epēshīn Forum, a chamber closed to non-senators, who are forbidden even to speak at its sessions.

Beneath the Surīhurūk's office churns the ongoing rivalry of the Eshbrīn and Orondēs parties, with the Lektān priesthood lending its moral weight to the latter — and a handful of lesser city folk the party has come to know along the way.

members.name: Hethel
members.role: Surīhurūk presiding over the hundred senators

members.name: Kir-Hamash
members.role: low-level clerk in the Curia; a contact for tracking down El's missing advisor

members.name: Amat
members.role: silversmith of the Marble Quarter; bought the party's silverware for 880 coin

members.name: Buddy
members.role: charmed sentry; ignorance is bliss

name: The Allhammers
summary: Once the order the party belonged to, the Allhammers are monster-hunters and peacekeepers sworn to the Coppersmith, based out of the Winehall in Summarch. After Grandmaster Hagar's murder, Rennick was elected in his place — and used the office to overthrow Summarch's ruler Selwin at the Siege of the Seat, only to reveal that he had merged his spirit with the Coppersmith himself in the Return of the Kembar.

Shiv Mathor chose to stand at Rennick's side and was later raised to Steward; the rest of the company refused and fled, their anvil pendants cracking and rusting the moment they were cast out. The order now serves Rennick's new kingship, its former independence gone.

members.name: Rennick
members.role: Grandmaster of the Allhammers; merged his spirit with the Coppersmith; self-proclaimed King of Westerland

members.name: Shiv Mathor
members.role: Steward of the Allhammers; betrayed the party to stand at Rennick's side

members.name: Finch
members.role: Marshal of the Allhammers, a Dwarf

members.name: Leon Mallister
members.role: formerly of the Allhammers

members.name: Frösted Flækes
members.role: formerly of the Allhammers

members.name: Babar Ett-Clan
members.role: formerly of the Allhammers

members.name: Guantanamo Michael
members.role: formerly of the Allhammers

members.name: Eol Firenze
members.role: formerly of the Allhammers; remained in Relkor to stall Rennick

name: The Ninth House
summary: A secret order unknown even to the Eight Houses, the Ninth House was founded by Doremil the Raven just before their self-sacrifice completed the Binding, with the sole purpose of ensuring the Kembar never return to the Wide World. Its cells operate in near-total isolation from one another, taking rare orders from a hidden 'handler' further up the chain.

Aderyn Sly is a Ninth House agent, as was Eol Firenze before he stayed behind in Relkor to stall Rennick. In Epēshu, Aderyn's contact Pahīn sheltered the party in a safehouse — until news of the Kembar's return, unheard-of even within the House, sent him rushing to inform his superiors in person.

members.name: Aderyn Sly
members.role: Ninth House handler; ancient Wite

members.name: Eol Firenze
members.role: Ninth House agent placed among the Allhammers

members.name: Pahīn
members.role: Aderyn's contact in Epēshu; sheltered the party in an abandoned villa

name: The Cloister
summary: The Cloister is the order of herbalists, botanists, poisoners, and alchemists sworn to Mālgal the Shepherd. Frösted Flækes counts himself among their number, an identity he rarely advertises.

In Epēshu's Marble Quarter, the alchemist Dravidian Chasal — a fellow native of Rhusagos — recognized Frösted as both a Cloister member and the inventor of the Frösted Flask after the two spoke in their shared tongue, striking a lucrative deal for invisibility potions on the strength of that kinship.

members.name: Frösted Flækes
members.role: alchemist of the Cloister; inventor of the Frösted Flask

members.name: Dravidian Chasal
members.role: Elf alchemist of the Marble Quarter; recognized Frösted as Cloister and Allhammer

name: Pebros's Army
summary: The army of Epēshu marches under the banner of General Pebros, informal leader of the Orondēs conservatives. Having been away on campaign for some time, it is now encamped among the ruins of Lepon, some fifty miles from the city, as Pebros makes his way home.

The army's loyalty lies with Pebros personally rather than with the senate, a fact that has the Eshbrīn populists uneasy — and which the Orondēs may yet use to reclaim the government by force of arms.

members.name: Pebros
members.role: commander of the army encamped at the ruins of Lepon

name: The Māgosh
summary: The Māgosh are the eight great city-states of Patrinor, of which Epēshu is one, alongside Cyrikon and Bōlkhar in Rubea, Hordon, Drāmūz, and Tamaron on the Leponnian Peninsula, and Gizalīs and Ākat in the eastern land of Makagos. Lepon was once numbered among them before its sack and ruin.

Epēshu itself contests dominion over the Marble Gulf with Hordon, and holds sway over the lands of Pēshunor and Lamor — a position of Māgos-level influence that shapes everything the party has found in the city, from its senate to its priesthood. Captain Lahar, the Patrin sea-captain who ferried the party across the White Sea to Epēshu, hails from this same world of Māgos rivalries and trade.

members.name: Captain Lahar
members.role: Patrin sea-captain who ferried the party to Epēshu, now blind and left behind at port
